items.mistake: Reporting GridSearchCV best_score_ as final unbiased test performance
items.fix: Use nested CV outer scores for generalization estimates; reserve inner best_score_ for development ranking only.
items.why: Inner CV both selects among many settings and scores them on the same folds—best inner mean is optimistically biased.
items.example: 40-config RF grid, inner mean AUC 0.91 reported to stakeholders; outer nested mean AUC 0.84 on the same data.

items.mistake: Picking the model with highest mean CV score but very high fold std
items.fix: Weigh stability: prefer comparable mean with lower std, or use repeated CV to confirm the winner.
items.why: High spread indicates the model overfits certain partitions—mean alone hides overfit-prone variance.
items.example: Depth-20 RF mean AUC 0.845 (std 0.06) beats depth-8 mean 0.842 (std 0.01)—depth 8 generalizes more reliably.

items.mistake: Tuning on the locked test set after CV disagrees slightly
items.fix: Keep all hyperparameter and model decisions on train+val CV; evaluate the final refit once on test.
items.why: Each test peek converts the test set into a validation set and overfits the reporting metric.
items.example: CV picks α=1; test RMSE worse than α=0.1 on test—re-tuning on test and reporting the retry inflates claims.

items.mistake: Using CV model selection without refitting on full development data
items.fix: After CV picks hyperparameters, refit on all train+val rows before test evaluation or deployment.
items.why: Each CV fold trains on a subset; deployment should use all available development labels unless hold-out policy forbids it.
items.example: Best ridge α found by CV but model deployed from the last fold's 80% train subset—wastes 20% of dev labels.
